concepts.title: 1^∞ form
concepts.conceptBody:
Indeterminate form ${{1}^{\infty }}$

When base $\to 1$ (but no equal to 1 ) and power $\to \infty $, the form is called Indeterminate form ${{1}^{\infty }}$.

Example : $\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to \frac{\pi }{2}}}{{(\text{sin}x)}^{\text{tan}x}}\left[ {{1}^{\infty }} \right.$ form $]$

concepts.title: 0^0 form
concepts.conceptBody:
Indeterminate form ${{0}^{0}}$

When base $\to 0+0$ (but base is not equal to zero) and power $\to 0$ (but power is not equal to zero), the form is called the indeterminate form ${{0}^{0}}$.

Example : $\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}{{(\text{sin}x)}^{\text{tan}x}}\left[ {{0}^{0}} \right.$ form $]$

concepts.title: ∞^0 form
concepts.conceptBody:
Indeterminate form ${{\infty }^{0}}$

When base $\to \infty $ and power $\to 0$ (but power is not equal to zero), the form is called the indeterminate form.

Example : $\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}{{(\text{cot}x)}^{\text{sin}x}}\left[ {{\infty }^{0}} \right.$ form $]$

concepts.title: Equating the Limit to A and taking log on both sides
concepts.conceptBody:
Miscellaneous Forms 

(I) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{0}^{0}}$ form : When $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to \text{a}}}\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\ne 1$ but $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is positive in the neighbourhood of $\text{x}=\text{a}$.

In this case we write, \[{{\left. \left\{ (\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right. \right\}}^{\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)}}={{\text{e}}^{\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{\text{e}}}{{\{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\}}^{\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)}}}}\]

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to a}}{{[f\left( x \right)]}^{g\left( x \right)}}={{e}^{\text{li}{{\text{m}}^{x\to ag\left( x \right)\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{e}}f\left( x \right)}}}}$

Illustration

Evaluate $li{{m}_{x\to {{0}^{+}}}}{{(sinx)}^{x}}$ 

Solution: 

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Let }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }A=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\text{sin}x)}^{x}}$

$\Rightarrow $ $~\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ logA }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\underset{\text{x}\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\text{xlog}\left( \text{sinx} \right)$

$\text{logA }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\underset{\text{x}\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\text{log}\left( \text{sinx} \right)}{1/\text{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\![\!\!\text{ By }\!\!~\!\!\text{ L }\!\!'\!\!\text{ Hospital }\!\!'\!\!\text{ s }\!\!~\!\!\text{ rule }\!\!]\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

=$~\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\frac{1}{\text{sin}x}\cdot \text{cos}x}{-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}=-\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{x}^{2}}\text{ cot}x$

$~=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{\text{tan}x}=0$

$\Rightarrow $ $~\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}=1\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\underset{\text{x}\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\text{sinx})}^{\text{x}}}=1$

Illustration

Evaluate \[li{{m}_{x\to 0}}{{(cosecx)}^{x}}\] 

Solution: 

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Let }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }A=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\text{cosec}x)}^{x}}$ $\left( {{\infty }^{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

\[\text{log}A=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,x\text{log}\left( \text{cosec}x \right)\]

$\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\text{log}\left( \text{cosec}x \right)}{\frac{1}{x}}$ $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \frac{\infty }{\infty }\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$~=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{1}{\text{cosec}x}\cdot \frac{\left( -\text{cosec}x\text{cot}x \right)}{-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\![\!\!\text{ By }\!\!~\!\!\text{ L }\!\!'\!\!\text{ Hospital }\!\!'\!\!\text{ s }\!\!~\!\!\text{ rule) }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{\text{tan}x}=0$

$\therefore $ $\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{x}}=0$ or $A=1$

$\Rightarrow $ $~\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\text{cosec}x)}^{x}}=1$

Illustration

Evaluate $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}{{e}^{\frac{1}{xlogx}}}$ 

Solution: 

Let $A=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}{{e}^{\frac{1}{x\text{log}x}}}$

$\text{log}A=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{1}{x\text{log}x}\cdot \text{log}{{e}^{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{1/x}{\text{log}x}\cdot \text{log}{{e}^{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \frac{\infty }{\infty } \right.$ form $)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ [By L'Hospital's rule]

$=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{-1/{{x}^{2}}}{1/x}=-\infty $

$\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{\text{e}}}\text{A}=-\infty $

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }A={{e}^{-\infty }}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{1}{{{e}^{x\text{log}x}}}=0$

Illustration

Evaluate $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}|x{{|}^{sinx}}$ 

Solution: 

$\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,|x{{|}^{\text{sin}x}}=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{e}^{\text{sin}x\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{e}}\left| x \right|={{e}^{\overset{\lim }{\mathop{x\to 0}}\,\frac{\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{e}}\left| x \right|}{\text{cosec}x}}}}}$

$={{e}^{\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{1/x}{-\text{cosec}x\text{cot}x}}}$

$~=\underset{\text{x}\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,-\frac{\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}{\text{xcos}x}={{\text{e}}^{\text{lim}\to 0}}-{{\left( \frac{\text{sin}x}{\text{x}} \right)}^{2}}\cdot \left( \frac{\text{x}}{\text{cos}x} \right)$

$~={{\text{e}}^{-\left( 1 \right)\cdot \left( 0 \right)}}={{\text{e}}^{0}}=1$

Illustration 

Solve $li{{m}_{x\to {{0}^{+}}}}lo{{g}_{sinx}}sin2x$ 

Solution: 

Here, $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to {{0}^{+}}}}\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{\text{sin}x}}\text{sin}2x$

\[~=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\text{logsin}2x}{\text{logsin}x}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \frac{-\infty }{-\infty }\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)\]

\[~=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\frac{1}{\text{sin}2x}\cdot 2\text{cos}2x}{\frac{1}{\text{sin}x}\cdot \text{cos}x}=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\left( \frac{2x}{\text{sin}\left( 2x \right)} \right)\text{cos}2x}{\left( \frac{x}{\text{sin}x} \right)\text{cos}x}\] [By L'Hospital's rule]

\[~=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\text{cos}2x}{\text{cos}x}=1\]

Illustration 

Solve $li{{m}_{x\to {{0}^{+}}}}{{(sinx)}^{tanx}}$ 

Solution: 

Here $\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\text{sin}x)}^{\text{tan}x}}$ (${{\mathrm{0}}^{\mathrm{0}}}\mathrm{form}$)

Let $A=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\text{sin}x)}^{\text{tan}x}}$

Taking $\text{log}$ on both sides, we get

$\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{e}}A$ $~=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\text{tan}x\text{log}\left( \text{sin}x \right)$

$~=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\text{log}\left( \text{sin}x \right)}{\text{cot}x}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \frac{-\infty }{-\infty }\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$ [By L'Hospital's rule]

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Applying }\!\!~\!\!\text{ L-Hospital }\!\!'\!\!\text{ s }\!\!~\!\!\text{ rule }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{1}{\frac{\text{sin}x}{-\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}}\cdot \text{cos}x=\underset{x\to {{0}^{+}}}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,-\text{sin}x\cdot \text{cos}x=0$

$\therefore $ $~\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ logeA}=0$

$\Rightarrow $ $\text{A}={{\text{e}}^{0}}=1\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ $\Rightarrow $ $\text{A}=1$

Illustration

Evaluate $li{{m}_{n\to \infty }}{{(\pi n)}^{2/n}}$ 

Solution: 

Here; $A$ $~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{(\pi n)}^{2/n}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( {{\infty }^{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$\text{log}A$ $~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{2\text{log}\left( \pi n \right)}{n}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \frac{\infty }{\infty }\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{2\cdot \frac{1}{\pi n}\cdot \pi }{1}=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{2}{n}=0$

$\text{loge }A$ $=0\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ $\Rightarrow $ $A=1$

Illustration

Evaluate $li{{m}_{n\to \infty }}{{\left( \frac{{{e}^{n}}}{\pi } \right)}^{1/n}}$ 

Solution: 

Here, $A$ $~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{\left( \frac{{{e}^{n}}}{\pi } \right)}^{1/n}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( {{\infty }^{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$\text{log}A$ $~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{1}{n}\text{log}\left( \frac{{{e}^{n}}}{\pi } \right)$

$~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{n\text{log}e-\text{log}\pi }{n}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \frac{\infty }{\infty }\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$~=\underset{n\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\text{log}e-0}{1}$

$~=\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{{}}}e$

$\Rightarrow $A = e

examples.title: If $a, b, c, d$ are positive, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(1+\frac{1}{a+b x}\right)^{c+d x}=$
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1+\tan x}{1+\sin x}\right)^{\operatorname{cosec} x}$ is equal to
